assertions.expectation: Identifies this as an admission webhook compatibility issue — the webhook is rejecting pod creation requests
assertions.reason: Response correctly identifies this as a cert-manager admission webhook failure blocking pod creation after Kubernetes upgrade

assertions.expectation: Provides commands to identify the problematic webhook (kubectl get validatingwebhookconfigurations / mutatingwebhookconfigurations)
assertions.reason: Includes 'kubectl get validatingwebhookconfigurations | grep cert-manager' command to identify the problematic webhook

assertions.expectation: Offers an immediate fix: temporarily disable or remove the problematic webhook
assertions.reason: Provides kubectl patch command to set failurePolicy to Ignore as immediate mitigation

assertions.expectation: Provides the kubectl delete command for the webhook configuration as emergency mitigation
assertions.reason: Response uses kubectl patch to change failurePolicy but does not provide kubectl delete command for the webhook configuration

assertions.expectation: Warns that deleting the webhook removes validation — it's a temporary fix
assertions.reason: Response explains this is temporary mitigation and later shows how to restore validation, noting that it allows pods to create while fixing the root cause

assertions.expectation: Recommends checking cert-manager version compatibility with Kubernetes 1.32
assertions.reason: Includes specific commands to check current cert-manager version and search for versions compatible with Kubernetes 1.32

assertions.expectation: Advises upgrading cert-manager to a version that supports 1.30 as the permanent fix
assertions.reason: Provides helm upgrade commands to upgrade cert-manager to a compatible version as the permanent solution

assertions.expectation: Mentions setting failurePolicy to Ignore as a less drastic alternative to deletion
assertions.reason: Uses failurePolicy Ignore as the primary mitigation strategy rather than webhook deletion

assertions.expectation: Includes commands to verify pods can be created after the fix
assertions.reason: Provides 'kubectl run test-pod' command to verify pod creation works after applying the fix

assertions.expectation: Notes that any webhook configuration changes (failurePolicy, cert-manager version) must be persisted in the source of truth (Helm chart, GitOps manifests, CI/CD pipeline) to survive redeployment
assertions.reason: Response does not mention persisting changes to source of truth like Helm charts or GitOps manifests

assertions.expectation: Qualifies failurePolicy: Ignore as a temporary debugging measure with explicit risks, recommending revert to Fail after the root cause is resolved
assertions.reason: Response explicitly shows reverting failurePolicy back to Fail after upgrading cert-manager and explains it's temporary mitigation
